what_it_does: This file defines a React workspace component, QaWorkspace, that renders a Visual QA interface. It displays an approved canonical mockup beside a prototype screenshot, overlays SVG drift annotations on the prototype, lists pass/warn/fail QA flags, shows a nearest rejected anti-pattern and a style anchor, and presents a recommended set of fixes. It also defines two helper components: FlagRow for individual flag entries and DriftOverlay for the SVG annotation layer.
observations: Uses a React import alias: const { useEffect: useEffectQ } = React.
Reads shared data from window.MS_DATA and finds mockups by hardcoded ids: m_ci_v2, m_sa_1, m_r_1.
Calls window.ViContext.set({ workspace: 'qa' }) inside a useEffect on mount.
Renders a two-column grid comparing the canonical mockup and the prototype screenshot.
The prototype image uses the same target.src as the canonical but applies a CSS filter (hue-rotate/saturate).
DriftOverlay draws three percentage-based SVG rectangles/text labels over the prototype.
QA flags are rendered with FlagRow components using tone pass/warn/fail and a region chip.
Includes panels for nearest rejected pattern, style anchor, and a ViNote with numbered fix recommendations.
Mixes inline styles with CSS utility classes like col, row, gap-3, mt-6, ws-head__eyebrow.
No propTypes, TypeScript, or explicit key props on the FlagRow list.
standouts: SVG-based drift overlay with percentage coordinates and dashed strokes.
Side-by-side canonical vs prototype comparison layout.
Explicit linkage to a rejected anti-pattern and a style anchor alongside the QA flags.
ViNote that enumerates concrete fixes to send back to Claude Design.
conventions: Functional React components with destructured props.
Global window objects for application data (MS_DATA) and context (ViContext).
CSS utility classes combined with inline style objects.
Ternary expressions mapping tone to icon and CSS class names.
concerns: The prototype screenshot reuses the canonical image source with only a CSS filter, so it is not actually a separate exported prototype screenshot.
SVG overlay coordinates are hardcoded percentages and may misalign on different image sizes or aspect ratios.
No guard against missing mockups (find could return undefined).
No accessibility attributes on the SVG annotations.
FlagRow list items lack React key props.
Heavy coupling to global window globals makes the component hard to test in isolation.
notes: A file-local visual QA view that compares a canonical mockup to a prototype, overlays drift regions, and packages fix recommendations.
questions: Where are Btn, Chip, Panel, MockThumb, Icon, and ViNote defined?
Is target.src intentionally reused for both canonical and prototype, or should the prototype load a different screenshot?
How are the SVG overlay percentages calibrated to the actual prototype image?
What is the schema of window.MS_DATA and how are mockup ids assigned?
Why is useEffect imported as useEffectQ?
What does the onGoTo prop do, since it is not used inside the component?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to compare an approved mockup against a Claude Design prototype screenshot and rejected anti-patterns; the file does render that comparison interface, flags drift, and suggests fixes, so the purpose matches the implementation.
apparent_themes: Visual regression and design-to-code fidelity review
Canonical source of truth versus generated output
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to create a structured visual QA workflow where a reviewer can spot specific design drift, see what rejected pattern the drift resembles, reference a style anchor, and send a concise fix list back to the generative design tool.
novelty: It combines a canonical mockup, a prototype screenshot, an SVG drift annotation overlay, a rejected-pattern reference, a style-anchor reference, and an AI-authored fix recommendation into a single QA workspace view.
contribution: It contributes a concrete UI pattern for visual QA review: annotated drift overlays, severity-coded flag rows, rejected-pattern/style-anchor context panels, and an actionable recommendation note that can be forwarded to a design tool.
summary_for_embedding: A React workspace component that renders a side-by-side visual QA interface comparing an approved canonical mockup with a prototype screenshot, overlaying SVG drift annotations and listing pass/warn/fail flags plus rejected-pattern and style-anchor references.
intention_for_embedding: To let a reviewer detect and communicate specific design drift between a canonical mockup and a generated prototype so fixes can be sent back to the design tool.

symbols.does: Renders a single QA flag row with an icon, title, body, and region chip based on a pass/warn/fail tone.
symbols.description: FlagRow is a small presentational component that maps a tone prop to an icon and CSS class, then displays the flag title, explanatory body, and a region chip in a flex row. It is used inside the QA flags panel to list each detected drift item.

symbols.does: Renders an SVG overlay with dashed rectangles and labels marking drift regions on the prototype screenshot.
symbols.description: DriftOverlay is a fixed-position SVG component that draws three percentage-based annotation boxes over the prototype image: a broken trace line region, a source-rows density region, and a Vi note off-grid region. It uses color-coded strokes and text labels to visually flag where the prototype diverges from the canonical mockup.
